insight: For France, hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they have slightly lost momentum during these breaks, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, indicating a minor decline in their performance. However, the numbers also suggest that when France was pressing before a break, they struggled to maintain their momentum afterwards, losing it at an average rate of -0.300 per minute, whereas when they weren't pressing, they actually gained momentum at a rate of +0.200 per minute, which could indicate that they benefit from a more relaxed approach leading into a break. This implies that France's strategy and performance leading into a hydration break can significantly influence their ability to maintain or gain momentum afterwards.
